channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Gameplay Makes Fans Wary of Low Traffic Numbers
item.content:encoded:
Forza Horizon 6 has a brand-new gameplay video that’s doing the rounds, but it’s not being met with the best reception. The video, which showcases nearly 10 minutes of raw footage of the open world in Japan, raises a major concern.

It looks almost like there’s no traffic on the roads. In the comments under the video, prospective fans of this year’s Forza Horizon offering are debating the ‘dead city’ concept and suggesting that Playground Games needs to massively ramp up the traffic density in this title.

Will Forza Horizon 6 Have Painful Traffic Mechanics?

There’s something exhilarating about zipping down the road in Forza Horizon games, sitting behind the wheel of a souped-up supercar and whipping in and out of traffic.

However, the latest Forza Horizon 6 gameplay video to surface suggests that players might be doing a little less of that in this game, which is set to drop on May 19.

The exclusive, first-look video was uploaded by IGN recently, and it showcases nearly 10 minutes of footage. However, you can probably count on one hand the number of ‘other cars’ on the road. It doesn’t matter if the player is on country lanes, sprawling highways, or dense, urban tracks; the number of cars doesn’t really change.

It’s glaringly noticeable in some sequences. These inter-city highways that should theoretically accommodate thousands of cars have two or three at the most.

Sure, Forza Horizon 6 looks gorgeous, and the world of Japan has been faithfully recreated, but fans are complaining that it feels dead.

Do you have a problem with fewer cars making up the traffic palette in Forza Horizon 6? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Marathon might come to PS4 and Xbox One

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#999)—Answers for Friday, March 6
item.content:encoded:
This is it! It’s the last day you’ll ever see NYT Connections puzzles in the triple digits! Tomorrow, we hit the 1000th NYT Connections puzzle and, quite honestly, things will never be the same after that. Well, they probably will, but at least you can say you were here for the milestone!

Before we get to the big 1000, it’s time for NYT Connections #999. Below, I’ve got the best NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to ace today’s challenge.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (March 6)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • SPONGE
  • MARBLE
  • SHARP
  • WIT
  • POUND
  • LAYER
  • FACULTY
  • CURTAIN
  • HASH
  • PARASITE
  • BLANKET
  • SENSE
  • LEECH
  • CLOACK
  • NUMBER
  • MOOCH

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words you’d associate with a scrounger
  • Green – Things you might use to cover something up
  • Blue – Words you’d use to refer to the # sign
  • Purple – Words you’d associate with your mental capacity

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, March 6:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, March 6 (#999)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – LEECH, MOOCH, PARASITE, SPONGE
  • Green – BLANKET, CLOAK, CURTAIN, LAYER
  • Blue – HASH, NUMBER, POUND, SHARP
  • Purple – FACULTY, MARBLE, SENSE, WIT

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
